summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened a new series on building character by addressing the common sin of gossip. Using his personal story of recovery from a stroke and the metaphor of a tempting 'snack accident,' he illustrated how gossip, like a tasty treat, is appealing in the moment but spiritually destructive. The message centered on Proverbs 18:8, which describes gossip as 'choice morsels' that sink deep within. Pastor Travis taught that gossip feeds our fleshly insecurities while starving our spiritual maturity, acts as fuel for conflict, and is a hellish tool that separates friends. He distinguished gossip from its 'ugly cousin,' slander, emphasizing that God takes such speech seriously as it vandalizes the character of His children. The call was for personal growth through accountability, asking God to heal the heart issues that lead us to speak or listen to gossip, and ultimately to become more Christlike in our character and conversations.
mainPoints: Gossip is appetizing to the flesh but poisonous to the spirit, acting as spiritual junk food that corrupts hearts and breaks trust.
Gossip feeds our personal insecurities while starving our spiritual maturity, revealing whether we are trustworthy 'vaults' or untrustworthy 'volcanoes'.
Gossip is not merely harmful but hellish; it is a tool of the enemy that fuels conflict, destroys relationships, and is fiercely opposed by God.

versesMentioned.text: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; they go down to the inmost parts.
A wicked person listens to deceitful lips; a liar pays attention to a destructive tongue.
A gossip betrays a confidence, but a trustworthy person keeps a secret.
A gossip betrays a confidence; so avoid anyone who talks too much.
Likewise, the tongue is a small part of the body, but it makes great boasts. Consider what a great forest is set on fire by a small spark.
Without wood a fire goes out; without a gossip a quarrel dies down.
Those who consider themselves religious and yet do not keep a tight rein on their tongues deceive themselves, and their religion is worthless.
A perverse person stirs up conflict, and a gossip separates close friends.
Whoever would foster love covers over an offense, but whoever repeats the matter separates close friends.
Whoever conceals hatred with lying lips and spreads slander is a fool.

There are six things the Lord hates, seven that are detestable to him: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood, a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are quick to rush into evil, a false witness who pours out lies and a person who stirs up conflict in the community.
Whoever slanders their neighbor in secret, I will put to silence; whoever has haughty eyes and a proud heart, I will not tolerate.

Monday.scripture: Proverbs 18:8 (NIV) - 'The words of a gossip are like choice morsels; they go down to the inmost parts.'
Monday.action: Identify one situation today where you felt tempted to listen to or share a 'juicy' piece of information. Acknowledge the fleeting appeal.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me see gossip for what it is: tempting but toxic. Give me discernment to recognize its appeal and the strength to turn away.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 11:13 (NIV) - 'A gossip betrays a confidence, but a trustworthy person keeps a secret.'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a recent conversation. Were you a 'vault' that protected confidence, or a 'volcano' that leaked information? Commit to being trustworthy.
Tuesday.prayer: God, make me a trustworthy person. Help me to be a safe place for others, guarding confidences and building trust instead of breaking it.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 26:20 (NIV) - 'Without wood a fire goes out; without a gossip a quarrel dies down.'
Wednesday.action: Choose not to add 'wood' to a conflict today. If a conversation turns to gossip about someone, politely change the subject or walk away.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I don't want to be a fire-feeder. Help me have the courage to stop adding fuel to conflicts by refusing to engage in or spread gossip.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 16:28 (NIV) - 'A perverse person stirs up conflict, and a gossip separates close friends.'
Thursday.action: Think of one relationship that matters to you. Perform one intentional act today that builds up and protects that person, rather than tearing them down.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, guard my words. Let me be a person who brings peace and unity, not division. Help me use my speech to protect and honor my relationships.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 101:5 (NIV) - 'Whoever slanders their neighbor in secret, I will put to silence...'
Friday.action: Ask God to reveal any root of insecurity, jealousy, or hurt in your heart that might lead you to gossip. Journal one insight.
Friday.prayer: Healer God, I surrender my heart to you. Reveal the wounds and insecurities that make me want to tear others down. Heal me and make me secure in Your love.

Saturday.scripture: James 1:26 (NIV) - 'Those who consider themselves religious and yet do not keep a tight rein on their tongues deceive themselves, and their religion is worthless.'
Saturday.action: Share one key lesson from this week with a trusted friend or family member. Ask them to gently hold you accountable for your speech in the coming week.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill my heart and govern my tongue. As I prepare for worship, let my words be pleasing to You. I want my faith to be real and reflected in how I speak.
